what: hbsaems: Hierarchical Bayesian Area-Level Small Area Estimation Models — hbsaems-package
title: hbsaems: Hierarchical Bayesian Area-Level Small Area Estimation Models — hbsaems-package
text: hbsaems fits area-level Hierarchical Bayesian Small Area Estimation (HBSAE) models.  methodological foundation follows standard SAE literature – primarily Rao Molina (2015) – computational implementation adapts models parameterisation prior-specification conventions brms package (Buerkner 2017), targets Stan back-end.

title: hbsaems: Hierarchical Bayesian Area-Level Small Area Estimation Models — hbsaems-package
text: package implements standard area-level SAE models: Fay-Herriot normal (Fay & Herriot 1979), lognormal-lognormal positive, right-skewed responses         (Slud & Maiti 2006; & Chapman 2006), beta logit-normal proportions (Liu 2009;         Rao & Molina 2015, Sec.\ 8.2), binomial logit-normal counts trials. can augmented spatial random effects (CAR / SAR / BYM2; Besag et al.\ 1991, Riebler et al.\ 2016, Anselin 1988), nonlinear smooth terms (thin-plate splines, Gaussian processes), survey-design-informed fixed parameters (precision parameter \(\phi_i = n_i / \mathrm{deff}_i - 1\) beta model, \(\sigma_i = \sqrt{\psi_i}\) lognormal Fay-Herriot variant). Unit-level SAE models (e.g.\ nested error model Battese, Harter & Fuller 1988) focus package.

title: hbsaems: Hierarchical Bayesian Area-Level Small Area Estimation Models — hbsaems-package
text: facilitate principled Bayesian workflow advocated Gelman et al.\ (2020), hbsaems provides: Prior predictive checks via         prior_check; MCMC convergence diagnostics (Rhat, ESS, divergent         transitions) via convergence_check; Posterior predictive checks via         brms::pp_check() underlying brmsfit; Leave-one-cross-validation via loo; Bayesian model comparison via         model_compare / model_compare_all         Bayesian model averaging via model_average; Prior sensitivity analysis via optional         priorsense integration; Design-consistent benchmarking model-based         estimates direct estimates via         sae_benchmark; --sample prediction unsampled areas via         sae_predict.

title: hbsaems: Hierarchical Bayesian Area-Level Small Area Estimation Models — hbsaems-package
text: Three layers entry points, picked much customisation needed: Wrappers – hbm_lnln,         hbm_betalogitnorm, hbm_binlogitnorm.         SAE-friendly arguments (auxiliary, group,         n + deff, sampling_variance). Flexible factory – hbm_flex.  Works         registered family, exposes generic         fixed_params interface. Universal entry point – hbm.  Accepts         brms::bf() formula brms         (custom) family.

title: hbsaems: Hierarchical Bayesian Area-Level Small Area Estimation Models — hbsaems-package
text: Two custom brms families shipped (Loglogistic Shifted Loglogistic).  Users can register via register_hbsae_brms_custom; Stan code lives inst/stan/*.stan plain text loaded read_stan_function.

title: Loglogistic Distribution Functions — loglogistic
text: implementation follows canonical Wikipedia / flexsurv / eha parameterisation (Jackson 2016; Bennett 1983): $$Y \sim \mathrm{LogLogistic}(\mu, \beta),   \quad \mu > 0, \quad \beta > 0,$$ probability density function $$f(y \mid \mu, \beta) =   \frac{(\beta/\mu)(y/\mu)^{\beta - 1}}{\{1 + (y/\mu)^{\beta}\}^{2}},   \quad y > 0,$$ cumulative distribution function $$F(y \mid \mu, \beta) = \{1 + (y/\mu)^{-\beta}\}^{-1},$$ median \(\mu\), mean \(E[Y] = \mu \pi / [\beta \sin(\pi / \beta)]\) \(\beta > 1\).  Equivalently, \(\log(Y) \sim   \mathrm{Logistic}(\log\mu, \, 1/\beta)\). match brms lognormal convention?  brms::lognormal() family parameterises \(\mu\) log scale (\(\mu\) unconstrained uses identity link). log-logistic require redefining \(\mu = \log(\mathrm{median}(Y))\) – deviates every standard R reference (flexsurv, eha, Wolfram, scipy, Stata).  deliberately follow survival-analysis convention instead: \(\mu\) median (positive, log link), keeping interpretation simple posterior summaries comparable rest R survival ecosystem.

title: Power-Scale Prior Sensitivity Diagnostics for Fitted HBMs — prior_sensitivity
text: Prior sensitivity analysis answers question: “used slightly different prior, substantive conclusions change?”. power-scaling approach Kallioinen et al.\ (2023) detects: Prior–likelihood conflict: posterior moves     non-negligibly prior - -weighted.  Often     indicates overly informative misspecified prior. Weak likelihood: posterior dominated     prior.  Common SAE areas sampled units. Reported diagnostics include Kullback–Leibler divergence original posterior power-scaled posterior (prior, likelihood) categorical flag (prior-data conflict, strong prior, -). Computational cost.  re-sampling required: importance sampling reuses existing posterior draws.  Hence typical run costs seconds even large hierarchical models.

title: Power-Scale Prior Sensitivity Diagnostics for Fitted HBMs — prior_sensitivity
text: Always.  Specifically: every model fit, drawing substantive conclusions. Whenever convergence diagnostics         convergence_check() clean posterior         seems implausibly narrow implausibly wide. comparing models shrinkage priors – horseshoe         R2D2 informative, small differences         hyperparameters can move estimates noticeably.

title: Read the Stan Function Code for a Custom Distribution — read_stan_function
code: code <- read_stan_function("loglogistic") cat(code) #> // ============================================================================= #> // inst/stan/loglogistic.stan #> // #> // Stan function definitions for the Loglogistic distribution. #> // #> // Parameterisation: #> //   Y ~ Loglogistic(mu, beta) with mu > 0 (scale) and beta > 0 (shape). #> //   PDF: f(y) = (beta / mu) * (y / mu)^(beta - 1) / [1 + (y / mu)^beta]^2 #> //   CDF: F(y) = 1 / [1 + (y / mu)^(-beta)] #> // #> // Conventions: #> //   * VECTORISED signatures (neodistr convention: loop = FALSE in #> //     custom_family).  lpdf / lcdf / lccdf take vectors of y and mu and #> //     return a single accumulated log-density / log-CDF / log-CCDF. #> //   * rng remains scalar (one draw per call) since brms post-processing #> //     does the per-observation looping in R. #> //   * Function names match brms's required pattern: #> //       <name>_lpdf  /  <name>_lcdf  /  <name>_lccdf  /  <name>_rng #> // ============================================================================= #>  #> real loglogistic_lpdf(vector y, vector mu, real beta) { #>   int N = num_elements(y); #>   vector[N] z = y ./ mu; #>   // sum_i [ log(beta) - log(mu_i) + (beta - 1) log(z_i) - 2 log1p(z_i^beta) ] #>   return  N * log(beta) #>           - sum(log(mu)) #>           + (beta - 1) * sum(log(z)) #>           - 2 * sum(log1p(pow(z, beta))); #> } #>  #> real loglogistic_lcdf(vector y, vector mu, real beta) { #>   int N = num_elements(y); #>   vector[N] z = y ./ mu; #>   // log F(y_i) = -log1p(z_i^(-beta));  return sum over i. #>   return -sum(log1p(pow(z, -beta))); #> } #>  #> real loglogistic_lccdf(vector y, vector mu, real beta) { #>   int N = num_elements(y); #>   vector[N] z = y ./ mu; #>   // log(1 - F(y_i)) = -log1p(z_i^beta);  return sum over i. #>   return -sum(log1p(pow(z, beta))); #> } #>  #> real loglogistic_rng(real mu, real beta) { #>   // Inverse CDF method: q = mu * (u / (1 - u))^(1 / beta) #>   real u = uniform_rng(0, 1); #>   return mu * pow(u / (1 - u), 1.0 / beta); #> }

title: Benchmark Small-Area Estimates to Known Totals — sae_benchmark
text: predictions hbsae_results object produced sae_predict. target Numeric.  method = "ratio" "difference": single benchmark total \(T\).  method = "raking": numeric vector group totals (one per group; see groups). weights Optional numeric vector length equal number areas predictions.  Typical choices population sizes sampling weights.  Defaults equal weights (1 / n). method Character.  One : "ratio" Multiplicative:     \(\hat{\theta}_i^B = \hat{\theta}_i \times T /       \sum_j w_j \hat{\theta}_j\). "difference" Additive:     \(\hat{\theta}_i^B = \hat{\theta}_i +       (T - \sum_j w_j \hat{\theta}_j) / \sum_j w_j\). "raking" Iterative proportional fitting multiple     group totals.  Requires groups. groups Optional integer/character vector length equal number areas, assigning area benchmarking group. Required method = "raking"; ignored otherwise. posterior Optional logical matrix.  Controls Bayesian mode: NULL FALSE (default) Point-estimate mode –     behaviour v1.0.0. TRUE Bayesian mode.  Posterior draws extracted     predictions automatically (requires     predictions$model available). numeric matrix (\(D \times n\)) Bayesian mode     user-supplied draws (\(D\) draws, \(n\) areas). probs Numeric vector quantile probabilities summarise adjusted posterior (default c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975)). used Bayesian mode. max_iter Integer.  Maximum iterations raking (default 100L). tol Numeric.  Convergence tolerance raking (default 1e-8).

Fully Bayesian mode (posterior = TRUE supply     posterior matrix) Applies adjustment every     posterior draw recomputes SD, quantiles, RSE     adjusted draws.  statistically correct procedure     produces proper uncertainty intervals benchmarking.

title: Benchmark Small-Area Estimates to Known Totals — sae_benchmark
text: Benchmarking widely used official statistics ensure   consistency model-based small-area estimates published   aggregate totals reliable source.

title: Benchmark Small-Area Estimates to Known Totals — sae_benchmark
text: Applying deterministic adjustment factor posterior point   estimate distorts uncertainty structure: multiplicative   factor scales mean SD amount (  RSE percentage preserved), additive shift   change SD, RSE percentage shrinks small areas   grows large ones.  Neither post-hoc fixes   guaranteed correct general.  Bayesian mode every draw   benchmarked independently, resulting posterior carries   right uncertainty.

title: Benchmark Small-Area Estimates to Known Totals — sae_benchmark
text: Pfeffermann, D. (2013).  New important developments small area estimation.  Statistical Science 28(1), 40–68. Wang, J., Fuller, W. ., & Qu, Y. (2008).  Small area estimation restriction.  Survey Methodology 34, 29–36.

title: Benchmark Small-Area Estimates to Known Totals — sae_benchmark
code: # Synthetic predictions (point-estimate mode) p <- structure(   list(     result_table = data.frame(Prediction  = c(10, 12, 9, 11),                                SD          = c(1, 1, 1, 1),                                RSE_percent = c(10, 8, 11, 9)),     rse_model    = 9.5,     pred         = c(10, 12, 9, 11)   ),   class = "hbsae_results" ) bm1 <- sae_benchmark(p, target = 50, method = "ratio")  # Fully Bayesian mode with user-supplied draws set.seed(1) D <- 1000 draws <- matrix(rnorm(D * 4, mean = c(10, 12, 9, 11), sd = 1),                  nrow = D, byrow = TRUE) bm2 <- sae_benchmark(p, target = 50, method = "ratio",                       posterior = draws) bm2$result_table              # SD, RSE updated from draws #>   Prediction       SD RSE_percent     Q2.5      Q50    Q97.5 #> 1   47.61905 4.300884    9.031856 38.54522 47.34989 56.05428 #> 2   57.14286 4.312359    7.546629 48.62104 57.17462 65.55330 #> 3   42.85714 4.399850   10.266317 34.35734 42.75895 51.93634 #> 4   52.38095 4.229116    8.073767 43.66985 52.28960 60.65092

title: Generate Small Area Estimates — sae_predict
text: area \(= 1, \ldots, n\), function computes $$\widehat{y}_i = \frac{1}{S} \sum_{s=1}^{S} y_{}^{(s)}, \qquad       \widehat{\mathrm{sd}}_i^2 =       \frac{1}{S - 1} \sum_{s=1}^{S}       \left( y_{}^{(s)} - \widehat{y}_i \right)^2,$$ \(y_{}^{(s)}\) draws posterior predictive distribution \(S\) number draws.  relative standard error \(\mathrm{RSE}_i = 100 \cdot |\widehat{\mathrm{sd}}_i / \widehat{y}_i|\).

code: # \donttest{ library(hbsaems) library(brms) data("data_fhnorm") model <- hbm(   formula = brms::bf(y ~ x1 + x2 + x3),   data    = data_fhnorm,   chains = 2, iter = 2000, warmup = 1000, cores = 1,   seed = 123, refresh = 0 ) #> Warning: Model fitted without any area-level random effects. #>   This is unusual for Small Area Estimation: the standard Fay-Herriot model assumes u_i ~ N(0, sigma_u^2) per area, so estimates from a purely fixed-effects model will not borrow strength across areas. #>   Consider one of: #>     re = ~ (1 | area_id)                                     # IID area RE #>     spatial_var = 'area_id', spatial_model = 'car', M = W    # CAR spatial RE #>     spatial_var = 'area_id', spatial_model = 'sar', M = W    # SAR spatial RE #>   If a fixed-effects-only baseline is intentional, you can suppress this warning with `suppressWarnings()`. #> Compiling Stan program... #> Error in .fun(model_code = .x1): Boost not found; call install.packages('BH') est <- sae_predict(model) #> Error: object 'model' not found summary(est) #> Error: object 'est' not found plot(est, type = "predictions") #> Error: object 'est' not found plot(est, type = "uncertainty") #> Error: object 'est' not found # }

title: Shifted (3-Parameter) Loglogistic Distribution — shifted_loglogistic
text: implementation uses GEV-style parameterisation Hosking & Wallis (1997) Flood Estimation Handbook (Robson & Reed 1999), \(\mu\) pure location parameter (median), \(\sigma\) pure scale parameter \(\xi\) pure shape parameter: $$F(x \mid \mu, \sigma, \xi) =   \{1 + (1 + \xi z)^{-1/\xi}\}^{-1},   \qquad z = (x - \mu) / \sigma,$$ corresponding density $$f(x \mid \mu, \sigma, \xi) =   \frac{(1 + \xi z)^{-(1/\xi + 1)}}        {\sigma \{1 + (1 + \xi z)^{-1/\xi}\}^{2}}.$$ support depends \(\xi\): \(\xi > 0\): \(x \ge \mu - \sigma/\xi\) (bounded ). \(\xi < 0\): \(x \le \mu - \sigma/\xi\) (bounded ). \(\xi = 0\): \(x \\mathbb{R}\) (logistic limit). median always \(\mu\); mean exists \(|\xi| < 1\) \(\mu + \sigma (\alpha\csc\alpha - 1)/\xi\), \(\alpha = \pi\xi\).  Reducing , family contains: standard log-logistic \(\xi = 1\) (reparameterised); logistic distribution \(\xi \0\); generalised Pareto family \(\xi = -1\). parameterisation?  alternative "simple-shift" form, \(Y - \delta \sim \mathrm{LogLogistic}\), exists literature (Geskus 2001) closer spirit brms::shifted_lognormal()'s positive shift ndt.  deliberately follow GEV-style parameterisation provides smooth limit logistic         distribution \(\xi = 0\); parameters \((\mu, \sigma, \xi)\)         orthogonally interpretable (location / scale / shape); canonical form hydrology extreme-value         applications (Hosking & Wallis 1997).

title: hbsaems 1.0.0
text: First stable release. hbsaems fits area-level Hierarchical Bayesian Small Area Estimation models following methodological tradition Rao Molina (2015) doi:10.1002/9781118735855, computational implementation adapted parameterisation prior-specification conventions ‘brms’ package (Buerkner 2017 doi:10.18637/jss.v080.i01) Stan back-end. package designed support principled Bayesian workflow advocated Gelman et al. (2020) doi:10.48550/arXiv.2011.01808 – prior predictive checks, MCMC convergence diagnostics, posterior predictive checks, leave-one-cross-validation, Bayesian model comparison averaging, prior sensitivity analysis, design-consistent benchmarking part standard pipeline. entry consolidates changelog every development cycle since 0.1.2 maintenance release; package now follows semantic versioning 1.0.0 line constitutes stable user-facing function set whose signatures change v2.0.0.

title: hbsaems 1.0.0
text: Generic fixed_params mechanism hbm() hbm_flex(). Lets user pin distributional parameter value derived column, scalar, vector, formula evaluated data. Centralises pattern previously coded per-wrapper. hbm_betalogitnorm() refactor. Four operational modes: random ϕ\phi hyperprior; fixed ϕ\phi survey nn deff\mathrm{deff}; user-overridable hyperprior α,β\alpha,\beta via stanvars; generic fixed_params power users. Default priors filled automatically follow Liu (2009). hbm_lnln(sampling_variance = ...) Fay–Herriot lognormal model. Pins σi=ψi\sigma_i = \sqrt{\psi_i} known per-area sampling variance. Custom brms response distributions. Built-support Loglogistic Shifted Loglogistic, plus public extension framework (register_hbsae_brms_custom(), read_stan_function(), build_brms_custom_family()). Stan code lives inst/stan/ plain .stan files. registered family ships log-likelihood, posterior-predict posterior-epred hooks loo(), posterior_predict(), posterior_epred() work box. Spatial random effects. CAR (ICAR / proper / BYM2) SAR (lag / error) via spatial_var, spatial_model, car_type, sar_type, M. Weight matrices can constructed bundled build_spatial_weight() shapefile coordinates. Missing-data handling. Three strategies (deleted, multiple via mice, model via brms::mi()) auto-selection handle_missing = NULL. R2D2 (Zhang et al. 2022) selectable via prior_type. Splines: spline_k (basis dim) spline_bs (basis type: "tp", "cr", "cs", "ps"). GP: gp_k (Hilbert-space approximate GP basis dimension – Riutort-Mayol et al. 2023), gp_cov (covariance function: "exp_quad", "matern15", "matern25", "exponential"), gp_c (boundary-scale factor). Automatic warning exact GP (slow, O(n3)O(n^3)) requested 100 areas, recommended gp_k value. gp_scale deprecated favour gp_c; removal scheduled v2.0.0. Bilingual Shiny dashboard (run_sae_app()). English / Indonesian, dedicated spatial setup tab, -app code preview, CSV / RDS data upload. Source inst/shiny/sae_app/. Benchmarking helpers (sae_benchmark(), sae_predict()). Pfeffermann-style design-consistent benchmarking --sample prediction unsampled areas. Bayesian model averaging LOO weights. model_average() now accepts method argument: "manual" (default, user weights), "stacking", "pseudobma" (via loo::loo_model_weights, canonical Bayesian stacking / pseudo-BMA+ Yao et al. 2018). Power-scale prior sensitivity diagnostics via prior_sensitivity(): thin wrapper around priorsense::powerscale_sensitivity() (Kallioinen et al. 2024) detecting prior-data conflict weak likelihood fitted models.

text: Seven new vignettes covering full workflow: hbsaems-modelling (overview), hbsaems-lnln-model, hbsaems-betalogitnorm-model, hbsaems-binlogitnorm-model, hbsaems-spatial, hbsaems-handle-missing, hbsaems-run_sae_app. follow CRAN-safe pattern: heavy Stan fits evaluated build time; representative outputs printed illustrations explicit disclaimer. Three retained vignettes: complete-workflow, advanced-features, migration-guide.

title: hbsaems 1.0.0
text: posterior_interval() prior_draws() now re-export upstream generics   respectively, rather defining new generics conflicting signatures. fixes name-collision crash occurred  attached together  user called   object: error message  longer occurs. fix follows standard R package design pattern used  .

title: hbsaems 1.0.0
text: exported functions documented full roxygen2 blocks including @param, @return, @examples, references appropriate. Custom-distribution Stan code stored separate .stan files inst/stan/ (one source truth, syntax highlighting, string-escaping noise). Test suite reorganised CRAN-safe unit tests (tests/testthat/) heavy integration tests (tests/testthat/dev-tests/, gated skip_on_cran() excluded via .Rbuildignore).

title: hbsaems 0.1.0
text: Initial public release CRAN. Provided hbm(), hbcc(), hbmc(), hbpc(), hbsae(), three example datasets (data_fhnorm, data_lnln, data_betalogitnorm, data_binlogitnorm).
